one_line_thesis: AMD is gaining share in AI accelerators (Instinct MI300) and server CPUs (EPYC).

deep_dive.executive_summary:
AMD is gaining share in AI accelerators (Instinct MI300) and server CPUs (EPYC). FY2025 free cash flow per share was $3.37 on $6.5 billion continuing operating cash flow and $1.0 billion capital spending. Data Center revenue grew 32 percent to $16.6 billion with segment operating profit $3.6 billion. Proof-backed component base value is $96.45 per share, far below the live quote.

At ~$550, the base case yields −14.56% per year over seven years (bear −22.2%, bull −11.1%). The business has a stable moat and partial asymmetric payoff: AI upside is open, but the bear case includes custom silic...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Entry prices were computed mechanically from the routed power-zone profile (High-return compounder). They are decision inputs, not a decision; the owner must review the scenarios before acting.
pricing_analysis.strongest_counter_explanation: growth projected without its capital cost
pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: Per-share claims use fully diluted shares from valuation.inputs.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of AMD, including Data Center, Client/Gaming, and Embedded segment owner cash, net corporate liquidity, less AI competition and capex stress reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Three reportable segments valued through segment owner-cash discount on FY2025 FCF allocation; net liquidity and competition reserve are separate overlap keys.

economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K: stockholders' equity $63.0B; economic value in normalized owner cash ($3.3741/sh consolidated), not GAAP book alone.

component_groups.label: Data Center segment owner cash
component_groups.economic_claim: EPYC server CPUs and Instinct GPU AI accelerator normalized owner cash
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on segment-allocated FY2025 FCF per share ($1.62/sh).
component_groups.adjustments: MI300 ramp embedded in 16% years 1-5 growth path.

component_groups.label: Client and Gaming segment owner cash
component_groups.economic_claim: Ryzen PC and Radeon gaming/console normalized owner cash
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on segment-allocated FY2025 FCF per share ($1.42/sh).
component_groups.adjustments: PC cycle recovery uneven; conservative exit multiple.

component_groups.label: Embedded segment owner cash
component_groups.economic_claim: Xilinx adaptive SoC and embedded FPGA normalized owner cash
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on segment-allocated FY2025 FCF per share ($0.34/sh).
component_groups.adjustments: Highest segment margin; Xilinx synergy embedded in growth.

component_groups.label: Net cash and debt claims on common equity
component_groups.economic_claim: Net corporate liquidity after long-term debt
component_groups.valuation_basis: Net asset value on FY2025 filing-locked cash less debt.
component_groups.adjustments: Operating cash minimum judgment; no double-count with segment cash flows.

component_groups.label: AI competition, custom ASIC, and foundry capex stress reserve
component_groups.economic_claim: NVIDIA CUDA moat, hyperscaler custom silicon, foundry prepayment timing
component_groups.valuation_basis: Bounded negative reserve; not full enterprise value haircut.
component_groups.adjustments: Partial dhando: bear IRR -1.7% captures downside but reserve sizes competition risk.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: EPYC server CPUs and Instinct GPU AI accelerator normalized owner cash
valuation_proof.evidence: Primary bridge from AMD/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260204_rpt20251227_acc0000002488_26_000018.htm: FY2025 revenue $34639.0M (+34% YoY), continuing OCF $6490.0M, capex $970.0M, FCF $5520.0M, cash $5539.0M, debt $2348.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-23.
valuation_proof.adjustment: MI300 ramp embedded in 16% years 1-5 growth path.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

limitations: Segment FCF not separately disclosed; consolidated FCF allocated by revenue share.
AI accelerator share gains versus NVIDIA remain judgment bands.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism
method_fit.rule: Power Zones select the fitting economic method and eligible independent reviewers; they never choose the valuation answer or capital decision.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of AMD, including Data Center, Client/Gaming, and Embedded segment owner cash, net corporate liquidity, less AI competition and capex stress reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Three reportable segments valued through segment owner-cash discount on FY2025 FCF allocation; net liquidity and competition reserve are separate overlap keys.

insight_events.title: Net income up 164%

insight_events.recommended_follow_up: Verify the filing extract and reconcile the change with the model and prior period.

insight_events.title: Leadership / governance risk elevated
insight_events.summary: Leadership / governance risk elevated. AMD vs. Marvell Technology: Which Will Be the Next Trillion-Dollar Stock?; Is Marvell Technology Going to $1 Trillion?
insight_events.why_it_matters: Negative evidence. May change confidence in management alignment, oversight, or execution.
insight_events.recommended_follow_up: Check transaction context, trading plans, and whether the pattern is unusual for this issuer.

industry.structure: Advanced semiconductors is a concentrated oligopoly: NVIDIA dominates AI GPUs; Intel and AMD compete in x86 CPUs; TSMC fabricates most leading-edge chips. High R&D, long design cycles, and ecosystem lock-in (CUDA vs ROCm) create entry barriers.
industry.share_shift: AMD is gaining share in server CPUs (EPYC) and AI accelerators (Instinct MI300) as the credible second source; NVIDIA retains CUDA software moat; hyperscalers invest in custom ASICs.
industry.trend: AI data-center build-out drives demand for GPUs, HBM memory, and advanced packaging. Export controls limit China revenue. Foundry capacity allocation and capex step-ups are the main supply constraints.
